method: Official Apple sources and App Store artwork are used for structural measurement only. Third-party artwork is never supplied to the image model and is not republished on the approval board.

sources.label: Apple HIG: App icons
sources.why: Current June 2026 guidance for layered icons, simplicity, masks, appearances, and Icon Composer.

sources.label: Apple HIG: Motion
sources.why: Purposeful, optional motion and reduced-motion expectations.

sources.label: 2026 Apple Design Awards
sources.why: Current Apple-recognized design cohort. Recognition applies to the complete product, not the icon alone.

apps.rationale: Delight and Fun winner; tests whether a singular character can remain recognizable with very little geometry.
Interaction winner; useful for studying a centered symbol, dark appearance, and dimensional separation.
Visuals and Graphics winner; useful for studying a strong central motif and controlled depth.
Inclusivity finalist; tests a very simple silhouette across masks and small sizes.
A high-recognition wellness icon built from one oversized subject and a minimal number of marks.
Relationship category reference built from two large forms becoming one symbol.
Tests a distinctive central mark on a simple field, while showing why Better Together should avoid tiny text.
Tests a tightly cropped, personality-led subject with high shelf recognition.
A relationship product using bold repeated geometry and a singular seal-like silhouette.
Tests one recessed object, a restrained palette, and immediate small-size recognition.
